name: ntfy
description: Simple HTTP-based pub-sub notification service. Send push notifications to your phone or desktop via scripts.
summary: ntfy v2.22.0 (released 2026-04-21) is the latest release, focusing on a security fix for an SSRF vulnerability in the web push endpoint (GHSA-w9hq-5jg7-q4j7) and several web app bug fixes. Recent notable additions include S3-compatible attachment storage (v2.20.0), PostgreSQL read replica support (v2.19.0), verified email recipients via smtp-sender-verify (v2.21.0), and a major PostgreSQL backend rewrite (v2.18.0). Full release notes are available at https://github.com/binwiederhier/ntfy/releases.

answers.Is the project actively maintained?: Yes — multiple releases in March–April 2026 with active bug fixes and features
answers.Does it support authentication and acces: Yes — access tokens, user accounts, and permission-based topic access control are supported

breaking_excerpts: v2.20.0: ntfy will take full control over the attachment directory or S3 bucket — files matching the message ID format with no database entries will be deleted. Do not reuse the attachment-cache-dir for anything else.
v2.21.0: ntfy.sh now requires email address verification (smtp-sender-verify) before sending email notifications; unverified recipients will be rejected.
v2.22.0: Web push endpoint allow-list regex tightened to prevent SSRF (GHSA-w9hq-5jg7-q4j7) — previously unanchored patterns are now rejected.

alternatives.name: Pushover
alternatives.review: Paid SaaS, simple API, iOS/Android apps.

alternatives.name: Gotify
alternatives.review: Self-hosted only, real-time push via WebSocket.

alternatives.name: Apprise
alternatives.review: Apprise is a versatile open-source notification library supporting 80+ services but is a library/CLI tool rather than a standalone pub-sub server with its own push delivery infrastructure.

alternatives.name: Pushbullet
alternatives.review: Pushbullet provides cross-device notifications and file sharing with a polished UI but is a closed-source cloud service with a limited free tier and a history of feature degradation.

alternatives.name: Bark
alternatives.review: Bark is a lightweight open-source iOS-focused push notification tool that can be self-hosted but is limited to Apple devices and lacks the multi-platform reach of ntfy.
